verdicts.rationale: Evidence cites the whole file (1-152996), which matches the actual file length (verified: wc -l = 152996). Independently re-ran the counts on the raw source: pattern '^CLASS .* DEFINITION' = 1029 matches (grep -c, exact match to the claimed 1029); pattern '^INTERFACE\s' (declaration/deferred lines, excludes 'INTERFACES ...' usage lines since no whitespace follows) = 227 total, of which 113 contain 'DEFERRED' -> 227-113=114 non-deferred INTERFACE declarations, exactly matching the claimed 114.

Independently re-ran grep -c "CALL FUNCTION '" on the raw source: 558 matches, exact match. Read lines 82758-82763 and 83325-83330 directly: both pairs (82761-82762, 83328-83329) begin with '"' as the first non-blank character ('    "    CALL FUNCTION ...'), i.e. full ABAP comment lines, confirming these 4 occurrences are inactive inside METHOD zif_abapgit_object~jump next to the 'do not open a new window - so we revert to BDC' comment. Sentence's count and comment-exclusion claim both verified.
Independently re-ran grep for 'DEFINITION FOR TESTING' on the raw source: 0 matches, confirming the claimed zero occurrences (no ABAP Unit test classes in the standalone build).
Lines 7261-7264: INTERFACE zif_abapgit_version, CONSTANTS c_abap_version TYPE string VALUE '1.133.0'. Constant name and value match the sentence exactly.

Lines 152991-152996: INTERFACE lif_abapmerge_marker with comment '* abapmerge 0.16.8 - 2026-07-01T10:45:14.302Z' and CONSTANTS c_merge_timestamp/c_abapmerge_version matching those literal values exactly. The 'machine-generated concatenation, not hand-written ACME code' inference is directly supported by the abapmerge marker plus the MIT/abapGit license header visible in the same file (CL-006).

Lines 1-27 show REPORT zabapgit_standalone, '* See http://www.abapgit.org', MIT License header, 'Copyright (c) 2014 abapGit Contributors' -- matches the sentence exactly, including the explicit self-limiting scope ('proves provenance/licensing only, not functional purpose').

Lines 152967-152972: INITIALIZATION. PERFORM adjust_toolbar USING '1001'. lcl_password_dialog=>on_screen_init( ). START-OF-SELECTION. PERFORM run. matches exactly. Second evidence block 152480-152486 (METHOD on_screen_init) shows all five fields sc_title/sc_url/sc_user/sc_pass/sc_cmnt assigned literal texts, matching the sentence's list precisely.

Lines 152802-152820: TRY. IF zcl_abapgit_auth=>is_allowed( zif_abapgit_auth=>c_authorization-startup ) = abap_false. zcx_abapgit_exception=>raise(...). ENDIF. zcl_abapgit_migrations=>run( ). PERFORM open_gui. CATCH zcx_abapgit_exception INTO lx_exception. MESSAGE lx_exception TYPE 'E'. CATCH zcx_abapgit_not_found INTO lx_not_found. MESSAGE lx_not_found TYPE 'E'. ENDTRY. Every element of the sentence is directly present.

Lines 152822-152848 show IF sy-batch = abap_true -> zcl_abapgit_background=>run( ); ELSE branch: GET PARAMETER ID 'DBT' FIELD lv_mode, CASE lv_mode with c_action-go_db/go_home, lcl_startup=>prepare_gui_startup( ), zcl_abapgit_ui_factory=>get_gui( )->go_home( lv_action ), CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001. All elements of the sentence match.

Lines 23322-23340 show zcl_abapgit_html_viewer_gui with DATA mo_html_viewer TYPE REF TO cl_gui_html_viewer and a constructor parameter io_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container, confirming the wrapping. Lines 152844-152848 show 'CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001. " trigger screen', matching the comment text verbatim.

Lines 151580-151614 show TRY. enqueue( ). CATCH -> WRITE 'Another instance...'/RETURN; lt_list = zcl_abapgit_persist_factory=>get_background( )->list( ); LOOP AT lt_list; CREATE OBJECT li_log. Read the 5 omitted lines directly (within the same cited range, lines 151605-151611): TRY. CREATE OBJECT li_background TYPE (<ls_list>-method). li_background->run( ii_repo_online = li_repo_online ii_log = li_log it_settings = <ls_list>-settings ). CATCH cx_sy_create_object_error. -- confirms the dynamic instantiation and the run() call with online repo, fresh log (CREATE OBJECT li_log per iteration) and settings exactly as stated.

Lines 152827-152828 (IF sy-batch = abap_true. zcl_abapgit_background=>run( ).) directly support the 'unattended, background-schedulable execution' half of the pattern claim, but this citation alone does not show the 'job loop... configured pull/push actions per repository' detail (that detail is only shown under the separate CL-011 citation, not within this claim's own cited evidence).

Lines 152494-152509 show screen-input='0' forced only for P_URL/P_CMNT and screen-invisible='1' forced only for P_PASS; P_USER never appears in the SCREEN loop conditions. Lines 152531-152534 show CASE iv_ucomm WHEN 'OK' -> gv_confirm = abap_true (the confirm gate). Lines 152469-152474 show IF gv_confirm = abap_true. cv_user = p_user. cv_pass = p_pass. ELSE CLEAR. All elements of the sentence verified against the cited lines.

Lines 76560-76580 show LOOP AT lt_tadir ASSIGNING <ls_tadir>. TRY. delete_object(...). INSERT <ls_tadir> INTO TABLE lt_deleted. DELETE lt_tadir. lv_count = lv_count + 1. " make sure to save object deletions. COMMIT WORK. -- confirms COMMIT WORK executes once per successfully deleted object, inside the loop, immediately after the deletion, exactly as the bug-candidate describes.

Lines 61693-61695 show CLASS kHGwlHbtMQYaNXTWgQdwmgPrpXDFKn DEFINITION DEFERRED. / * renamed: zcl_abapgit_gui_page_flow :: lcl_filter / CLASS kHGwlHbtMQYaNXTWgQdwmgPrpXDFKn DEFINITION FINAL. -- confirms the rename mapping and comment placement exactly. Independently re-ran grep for '^\* renamed:' on the raw source: exactly 65 matches, matching the claimed whole-file count.

Line 38316 confirms the class name; lines 38411-38422 show METHOD create_alv. FIELD-SYMBOLS <lt_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE. ASSIGN mr_table->* TO <lt_table>. cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = ro_alv CHANGING t_table = <lt_table> ). -- matches the sentence's description of the factory call and generic table reference exactly.

Three cited lines each show an active CALL FUNCTION statement with the exact literal names: 146124 'BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL', 98578 'BAPI_ODSO_GETDETAIL', 101359 'BAPI_IOBJ_CREATE' -- all three literals confirmed present and not comments.

Lines 92651-92655: SELECT * FROM tadir INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_tadir WHERE pgmid = 'R3TR' AND object = 'SICF' AND obj_name LIKE lv_obj_name ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY. -- confirms the SELECT * is filtered by three WHERE conditions, not an unbounded scan, exactly as claimed.

Lines 99363-99374: CALL FUNCTION 'TR_ACTIVATE_NAMESPACE' EXPORTING iv_namespace = ls_nspc-namespace, then (after the sy-subrc check) UPDATE trnspace SET editflag = abap_true WHERE namespace = ls_nspc-namespace. -- both the FM call and the direct UPDATE are shown exactly as the sentence describes.

Lines 146150-146175: SELECT mandt FROM t000 INTO TABLE lt_dev_clients WHERE cccategory = lc_cc_category AND mandt <> sy-mandt; LOOP AT lt_dev_clients; SELECT SINGLE ... FROM usr21 AS u INNER JOIN adrp ... INNER JOIN adr6 ... CLIENT SPECIFIED WHERE u~mandt = <lv_dev_client> AND u~bname = iv_user ...; IF sy-subrc = 0. EXIT. ENDIF. -- every element of the sentence (bounded dev-client list, per-client CLIENT SPECIFIED join, exit on first hit) is directly present.

Line 95511 confirms the class name; read lines 95833-95847 directly (immediately preceding/overlapping the cited 95836-95845 range) to confirm 'METHOD zif_abapgit_object~jump.' is the enclosing method. Lines 95841-95845 show EXPORT scpr3_display_only = lv_display_only scpr3_bcset_id = lv_bcset_id TO MEMORY ID 'SCPR3_PARAMETER'. SUBMIT scpr3 AND RETURN. -- matches the sentence exactly.

Lines 717-724 confirm CLASS zcx_abapgit_exception DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cx_static_check ... INTERFACES if_t100_message. Line 1 of the file (REPORT zabapgit_standalone LINE-SIZE 100.) carries no MESSAGE-ID clause. Independently grepped the whole file for classic MESSAGE statements referencing a message class in parentheses (pattern MESSAGE x###(class)): all found classes are standard (oo, e2, 01, ms) with zero Z/Y-prefixed hits, and the only 'MESSAGE-ID' string in the file is inside an unrelated keyword-list literal at line 67104, not an actual REPORT clause -- confirms the sentence's coverage claim.

Lines 152574-152589 show the four INCLUDE ... IF FOUND statements (zabapgit_authorizations_exit, zabapgit_user_exit, zabapgit_background_user_exit, zabapgit_gui_pages_userexit) exactly as claimed. Searched the workspace's raw/ tree for files matching any of these four names: none found, confirming the 'none of these four include programs exist in this workspace's raw sources' assertion.

Lines 152448-152510: P_USER never appears in the on_screen_output SCREEN loop conditions (only P_URL/P_CMNT get screen-input='0' and P_PASS gets screen-invisible='1'), so it stays input-enabled by omission; line 152470 (cv_user = p_user.) inside IF gv_confirm = abap_true confirms the flow into the CHANGING parameter cv_user. Matches the sentence exactly.

Lines 152469-152510: IF gv_confirm = abap_true. cv_pass = p_pass. confirms the flow into cv_pass; lines 152506-152509 (IF screen-name = 'P_PASS'. screen-invisible = '1'.) confirm the masking without forcing screen-input='0', so the field remains editable while visually masked. Matches the sentence exactly.
Line 151589: WRITE: / 'Background mode'. -- a hardcoded string literal written unconditionally, matching the 'constant' kind classification exactly (no variable/DDIC source involved).

Lines 151595-151598: lv_repo_name = li_repo->get_name( ). WRITE: / <ls_list>-method, lv_repo_name. -- lv_repo_name is produced by a method-call assignment from a non-DDIC operand (the repo object), matching the 'computed' kind classification; <ls_list>-method comes from the background job list, not a raw DDIC field.
